payload.answer: Brain DB is the source of truth; Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection; Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Telegram addition and cheapest high-performing model remain open questions.
payload.decision: Store as high-level architectural guidance: Brain DB is source of truth, Cognee is a rebuildable semantic projection, Slack is a strict memory intake agent. Do not commit the open questions as decided facts.

entity_resolution.reason: No entity disambiguation is needed beyond preserving the named systems as described in the summary.

payload.intent: Extract durable memory from a chat summary about Brain architecture and note open questions without overcommitting.

memory_cards.statement: Brain DB should be the source of truth for memory state.
Cognee should function as a rebuildable semantic projection rather than the authoritative memory store.
Slack should be treated as a strict memory intake agent.
Whether Telegram should be added later is unresolved.
The cheapest model that still saturates performance is unresolved.

details: Preserved two unresolved questions as open items.
Avoided upgrading ambiguous items into commitments.

repair_options: If later decisions are made about Telegram, add them as a separate preference or decision card.
If a benchmark identifies the cheapest sufficiently performant model, record it as a decision with evidence and keep prior uncertainty notes only if still relevant.
